feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day, plus Polish public-holiday reminders

entry.title: The Uplift: The American Dream
entry.content: We celebrate the United States ahead of its 250th anniversary with stories about American heroes and pastimes. The founder of e.l.f. Cosmetics shares how he made a massive life change to become a Catholic priest.

entry.title: U.S. Servicemember Killed in Iraq as Troop Death Toll in Jordan Likely to Rise
entry.content: Separately, work is under way to identify human remains at a base in Jordan after a missile struck the location this weekend, U.S. Central Command said.

entry.title: Utility ETFs Soar 8% YTD on AI Power Demand. Here’s Which One to Buy
entry.content: Utility ETFs Soar 8% YTD on AI Power Demand. Here’s Which One to Buy

entry.title: How Fox kept cool at baked Birkdale to win The Open
entry.content: Ryan Fox wins the Open Championship - his first major title - with his final putt on a record-breaking weekend at Royal Birkdale.

entry.title: Fleetwood won't give up Open dream after 'special' week
entry.content: Tommy Fleetwood says he will continue to chase his dream of winning The Open after falling short at the end of a "special week" on home soil at Royal Birkdale.

entry.title: Official: Málaga announce new signing from Leganés
entry.content: La Liga newcomers Málaga have late this week announced a fresh addition to the club’s first-team ranks. This comes by way of confirmation that Juan Cruz has been brought in for the duration of the...

entry.title: Trump arrives at World Cup final for Argentina vs. Spain matchup
entry.content: Trump will watch final as he demands U.S. host World Cup again ‘immediately’

entry.title: Why Lamine Yamal doesn't wear No. 10 for Spain at World Cup
entry.content: It's a difference from his club time at Barcelona.

entry.title: World Cup final kick-off time delayed by performances and bizarre Tom Cruise speech
entry.content: The World Cup final between Argentina and Spain started six minutes late

entry.title: Game #99: Nationals at A’s Game Thread
entry.content: Two in a row?

entry.title: Arizona Cardinals training camp roster preview: OLB Baron Browning
entry.content: We will preview every player on the Cardinals roster leading up to training camp. This is about OLB Baron Browning.

entry.content: What Are Mineral Rights? The Complete Guide to Owning, Leasing and Selling Them

entry.title: SpaceX (SPCX) Stock Has Plunged 45% From Its Post-IPO High, and Cathie Wood Recently Bought $51 Million of It

entry.title: The next Wall Street shift is already underway
entry.content: The next Wall Street shift is already underway

entry.title: The soccer fans who fell in love with America during the World Cup
entry.content: The 2026 World Cup was an undeniable sensation in the United States, setting records for television ratings while millions of fans piled into stadiums to take in all the action.

entry.title: Justice Department activates untested court for 'alien terrorist' deportations
entry.content: The Justice Department has filed a first-ever petition to a secretive and dormant court created 30 years ago to weigh government requests to deport so-called “alien terrorists” from the United States

entry.title: Zelensky Seeks to Quell Growing Discontent Over Ouster of Defense Minister
entry.content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky held meetings with senior military commanders for a second day Sunday.

entry.title: Earthquake in Peru leaves at least six dead
entry.content: More than 30 injured and 300 displaced after buildings collapse near SicayaA 5.5-magnitude earthquake shook the Andes region of Peru, killing at least six people, authorities said Sunday. More than 30 people were injured and 300 have been displaced.The US Geological Survey reported the quake struck on Saturday at 9.24pm, with its epicentre located 2km (1.2 miles) west-south-west of the city of Sicaya in Huancayo province. It was 10km (6.2 miles) deep. Continue reading...

entry.title: Andy Biggs and David Schweikert offer competing visions for Republican Party in Arizona primary
entry.content: Rep. Andy Biggs and Rep. David Schweikert are clashing in the Republican primary for Arizona governor

entry.title: Becker: "Hardly a week goes by" without Trump administration threatening election official arrests
entry.content: CBS News contributors David Becker and Chris Krebs joined "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" in the wake of President Trump's primetime speech last week accusing China of accessing election data. Meanwhile, Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin said that if election officials do not secure elections, they could face fines or "depending on how far it goes, prison time." Becker said "hardly a week goes by where someone in this administration doesn't threaten election officials with arrest … it's become almost a running joke."

entry.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup final between Spain and Argentina kicks off Sunday
entry.content: Spain and Argentina will compete in the 2026 FIFA World Cup final at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey on Sunday.

entry.title: Transcript: Chris Krebs and David Becker on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," July 19, 2026
entry.content: The following is the transcript of an interview with CBS News contributors Chris Krebs and David Becker that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on July 19, 2026.

entry.title: Trump border czar says "I think body cameras are the way to go" after deadly ICE shootings
entry.content: Amid questions about immigration agents' actions in deadly shootings this month in Texas and Maine, Trump administration border czar Tom Homan told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that "I think body cameras are the way to go," adding that body cameras "let the American people know what that officer sees when he takes that action."

entry.title: Face the Nation: Hochul, Homan, Moynihan
entry.content: Missed the second half of the show? New York Gov. Kathy Hochul, White House border czar Tom Homan and Bank of America CEO Brian Moynihan join the show.

entry.title: I Can’t Stop Buying Alphabet Because The AI Talent Narrative is Wrong
entry.content: I Can’t Stop Buying Alphabet Because The AI Talent Narrative is Wrong

entry.title: Body found at Boston home owned by husband of Rep. Ayanna Pressley
entry.content: Boston police are investigating the discovery Saturday of a dead body that was found at a property that is owned by the husband of Congresswoman Ayanna Pressley.

entry.title: Homan says "I think body cameras are the way to go" after ICE shootings
entry.content: White House border czar Tom Homan said "I think body cameras are the way to go" after fatal shootings by federal immigration officers in Texas and Maine earlier this month.

entry.title: AI Is Keeping DRAM Prices High — Here’s Exactly When the Market Finally Loosens Up
entry.content: AI Is Keeping DRAM Prices High — Here’s Exactly When the Market Finally Loosens Up

entry.title: TechCrunch Mobility: The battle over robotaxi rules
entry.content: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

entry.title: Bank of America CEO Brian Moynihan says "affordability is a challenge" for customers
entry.content: Bank of America CEO Brian Moynihan told "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" that he has been hearing from customers that "affordability is a challenge" amid rising gas and food prices and inflation, but he added that "in the last couple months, we're seeing the wage growth of all income cohorts kind of coalesce together around 3 to 4% which is good."

entry.title: U.S.-Iran Fighting Escalates
entry.content: Plus, the CIA operative who helped the U.A.E. score an AI win, and Lionel Messi’s history with Spain.

entry.title: A couple with $6.1 million can't agree on when to retire — Ramit Sethi says 'it's not the numbers' holding them back
entry.content: A couple with $6.1 million can't agree on when to retire — Ramit Sethi says 'it's not the numbers' holding them back

entry.title: ‘Odyssey’ director Christopher Nolan calls AI an obvious ‘Trojan horse’
entry.content: "Everybody knows the Greeks are inside."

entry.title: Nearly half of retirees leave work earlier than planned — the 3 reasons Americans are retiring at 62 instead of 65
entry.content: Nearly half of retirees leave work earlier than planned — the 3 reasons Americans are retiring at 62 instead of 65

entry.title: A financial planner explains why splitting your partner's $2,100 mortgage will leave you with nothing. And it's okay

entry.title: Nonprofit Current AI is racing to build the World Wide Web of AI, free for all
entry.content: Current AI, a non-profit building AI that leaves no one culture behind, has made remarkable progress across devices, AI chat and more.

entry.title: Gleyber Torres homers for Lakeland, affiliates struggle overall
entry.content: Scores, news, and notes from around the Detroit Tigers farm system for Saturday, July 18, 2026.

entry.title: Wales beat Tonga in last warm-up for Commonwealths
entry.content: Wales' netballers head to the Commonwealth Games in high spirits after beating Tonga 65-52 in their final warm-up.

entry.title: Room for improvement but Scotland heading in right direction
entry.content: After wins against Argentina and Fiji and a loss against South Africa, Tom English reviews a productive summer for Scotland in the Nations Championship.

entry.title: Celtic favourites to sign Las Palmas goalkeeper as Spanish club drop €10m asking price
entry.content: Celtic are reportedly the favourites to sign Dinko Horkas from Las Palmas. The 27-year-old has caught the eye of the Celtic scouts and could make a move to Paradise to compete with Vil Sinisalo for th...

entry.title: Puka Nacua on whether he wants to stay with Rams: 'That's the plan'
entry.content: Puka Nacua was asked whether he'd like to stay in Los Angeles long-term and he made his intentions clear.

entry.title: How the World Cup has affected your Premier League team
entry.content: Once the trophy is lifted by either Spain or Argentina in New Jersey on Sunday night, attention will shift from the 2026 World Cup to the upcoming domestic season.

entry.title: Dante Moore continues to get love from analytics - USC has to stop him
entry.content: There is still much more to prove for Dante Moore, but USC's defense has a ton to prove as well. Reputations will be reshaped in Ducks-Trojans

entry.title: A retiree has $1 million and a $100K pension — here's why waiting until 70 for Social Security could be the smart play

entry.title: Spain vs Argentina prediction for World Cup final: Prediction markets for World Cup final
entry.content: Get a detailed Spain vs Argentina prediction to trade on markets for the 2026 World Cup Final.

entry.title: Is the World Cup ‘rigged’ for Argentina? Inside six moments that debunk 'VARgentina' narrative
entry.content: As Lionel Messi goes for his second straight FIFA World Cup championship, not everyone is thrilled about Argentina's run to the tournament's title match.

entry.title: Here's how to win a free Chipotle burrito during World Cup Final between Spain and Argentina
entry.content: 100,000 lucky fans will get a free entry at the chain restaurant.

entry.content: The Sporting News is tracking live scoring updates and hole-by-hole highlights for round one of the 2026 British Open. Follow along for updates from Royal Birkdale.

entry.title: The Open final round: Burns leads, Young charging, Fleetwood, DeChambeau and Scheffler among chasers
entry.content: Live radio commentary and text updates of the final round at Royal Birkdale as the champion golfer of the year is decided.

entry.title: What Would U.S. Families Sacrifice To End “Pay-To-Play” Youth Soccer?
entry.content: When a U.S. soccer team loses, fingers are aimed at the USA's "pay-to-play" soccer system. Other countries also pay, but they pay less. How could the USA make up the gap?

entry.title: NASCAR lineup for Sunday race at North Wilkesboro. Ryan Blaney gets the nod
entry.content: Sunday's race will be the first points-paying, regular-season Cup Series race at North Wilkesboro since NASCAR left there 30 years ago.

entry.title: Clairey Lin, 16-year-old from Canada, wins U.S. Girls' Junior
entry.content: Clairey Lin of Canada won the U.S. Girls’ Junior championship on Saturday, beating Amelie Zalsman of St. Petersburg, Florida, 5 and 4.

entry.title: England player ratings for entire World Cup: Wasted inclusion gets 2/10 but one star fetches 9/10
entry.content: A ‘what could have been’ tournament brought England semi-final heartbreak versus Argentina, then a thrilling win over France for bronze medals

entry.title: Tom Heaton predicts Senne Lammens’ reaction to World Cup mistake
entry.content: Manchester United veteran Tom Heaton has thrown his support behind Senne Lammens after the 23-year-old Belgian’s mistake at the World Cup, revealing how he will react to it next season.World Cup Hea...

entry.title: Hassan admits he will leave Oviedo, let’s hope Celtic is his destination
entry.content: Celtic target Haissem Hassan has admitted that he expects to leave his current side Real Oviedo during this transfer window with Celtic seemingly leading the chase for the exciting winger who impresse...

entry.title: British Open leaderboard 2026: Live golf scores, results from Open Championship Round 4
entry.content: Here's a look at the leaderboard for the 2026 Open Championship from Royal Birkdale.

entry.title: The Secret Mission Behind the U.A.E.’s AI Breakthrough
entry.content: Plus, pastors are turning to AI to write sermons, and the World Cup final is here.

entry.title: How Trump and his administration have mired the US in multiple crises
entry.content: From manufactured election insecurity to an outbreak of ‘explosive’ diarrhea, Americans are bearing the brunt of harmful policiesBetween widespread cuts to vital government agencies, various disease and illness outbreaks, and new environmental disaster vulnerabilities, the United States is facing a convergence of crises with widespread repercussions.Yet the Trump administration has hamstrung the federal government from addressing these crises – and in some cases is actively fanning them. Over the past year, the administration has moved to shrink the federal workforce, roll back environmental protections and policies intended to fight the climate crisis, reduced funding for scientific research and is seeking to advance legislation that would impose new voting restrictions. Continue reading...

entry.title: Violence repeatedly erupts in dementia care despite warnings, inspections show
entry.content: Nursing homes and assisted living facilities can be fertile ground for violence between residents with dementia, including fatal assaults. Court records and inspecitions how safeguards fall short.

entry.title: The U.S. Saw an Investment Boom in Venezuela. Now It’s a $37 Billion Problem.
entry.content: The Trump administration faces a daunting cleanup after earthquakes flattened the country’s infrastructure while oil and mineral reserves beckon to U.S. investors.

entry.title: A Portrait of a Beach City Defying Russia’s Attempts to Ensnare It
entry.content: The vital Ukrainian port of Odesa has many faces: a seaside resort, a center of culture and an economic prize coveted by the Kremlin.

entry.title: Russia launches deadly, massive ballistic missile attack in Kyiv, Ukraine
entry.content: One person was killed and 16 others wounded in the overnight Russian attack on Kyiv, local authorities said.

entry.title: A look back at the 2022 FIFA World Cup final
entry.content: As the 2026 World Cup draws to an end, take a look back at the memorable 2022 final match between Argentina and France.

entry.title: How do Spain and Argentina stack up? World Cup final teams by the numbers.
entry.content: Forty-eight nations​ competed in the largest FIFA World Cup in history and two are left standing, with Spain in search of its second World Cup title and Argentina shooting for back-to-back​ championships.

entry.title: Restoring Britain’s health to 2014 levels could add 2% to GDP, thinktank says
entry.content: Health Foundation paper argues health is an economic asset and improving it could generate £72bn for public financesRestoring the deteriorating health of the UK’s population to the level of 2014 would boost GDP by 2% and generate a £72bn dividend for the public finances, research suggests.A paper by the Health Foundation thinktank, published on Sunday, argues the nation’s health should be valued by policymakers as an economic asset. Continue reading...

entry.title: How to watch the 2026 FIFA Men's World Cup today
entry.content: Spain will face Argentina in the 2026 FIFA Men's World Cup final match on July 19. Here's how and when to watch the soccer game.

entry.title: Spain and Argentina's World Cup trophy count before 2026 final
entry.content: When Spain and Argentina face off Sunday in the World Cup final, it will mark just the second meeting between the two sides in the tournament's history, and their first in 60 years.

entry.title: Messi vs. Ronaldo GOAT scorecard: The ultimate year-by-year comparison
entry.content: Lionel Messi will play in his third World Cup final on Sunday, a match that feels like the definitive end of an era for soccer's great 21st-century individual battle.

entry.title: Who is singing the national anthems at the 2026 World Cup final? Here are the performers at MetLife Stadium
entry.content: Here's who's been announced to perform the national anthems before Spain faces Argentina.

entry.title: Who is Spain's goalkeeper? Meet Unai Simon, the stone wall with most shutouts in a World Cup
entry.content: Unai Simon has kept six clean sheets at this World Cup, the most by any goalkeeper in a single tournament, and has conceded just one goal across seven matches.

entry.title: How long is halftime at the World Cup final? Projected show length and more to know in 2026
entry.content: The 2026 FIFA World Cup final will feature the tournament's first-ever halftime show, making the break much longer than a standard soccer match.

entry.title: Spain vs. Argentina projected lineups, starting 11 for World Cup 2026 final at MetLife Stadium
entry.content: Forty-eight teams entered the 2026 FIFA World Cup, but only two remain alive for the title as Spain and Argentina meet at MetLife Stadium on Sunday.

entry.title: World Cup final pacific time kickoff: Spain vs. Argentina start time, schedule, channel, streaming to watch 2026 game live
entry.content: Here's everything you need to know about the World Cup final matchup between Spain and Argentina, including live streaming information, TV schedules, and kickoff details.

entry.title: Argentina roster projection without Lionel Messi: What 2030 World Cup could look like if soccer legend retires
entry.content: Here's what the 2030 roster for La Albiceleste could look like without Messi at the helm.

entry.title: World Cup final referee 2026: Who are match officials and VAR for Spain vs. Argentina?
entry.content: World Cup final referee 2026: Who are match officials and VAR for Spain vs. Argentina?

entry.title: Police say husband asked spellcasters to hex his now missing wife
entry.content: Shortly before Maya "May" Millete vanished, authorities say her husband Larry messaged a spellcaster, "Please punish May and incapacitate her enough so she can't leave the house."

entry.title: Timeline: The disappearance of Maya Millete
entry.content: Maya Millete, 39, went missing in January 2021. What was happening between her and her husband before she disappeared and what does spellcasting have to do with it?

entry.title: California man charged in missing wife's murder left a review for spellcaster
entry.content: Investigators say Larry Millete contacted spellcasters to try to put a hex on his wife Maya so she wouldn't leave him.

entry.title: US strikes Iran's Revolutionary Guard over attack that killed troops in Jordan
entry.content: The U.S. military said Sunday it had ended its latest round of airstrikes on Iran, which included targeting Iran’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard over an attack that killed American troops in Jordan

entry.title: Trump celebrates the World Cup as a US victory as he prepares to present trophy to the winning team
entry.content: President Donald Trump is set to present the World Cup trophy to the winner of Sunday’s final between Argentina and Spain

entry.title: São Tomé and Príncipe heads to polls in tense presidential election
entry.content: Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independentVoters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials.According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state’s elections, approximately 15% of whom live in the diaspora. Continue reading...

entry.title: US strikes Iran's Revolutionary Guard over an attack that killed troops in Jordan
entry.content: US strikes Iran's Revolutionary Guard over an attack that killed troops in Jordan

entry.title: 2 U.S. service members killed, 1 missing following Iranian strike on Jordan
entry.content: U.S. forces launched a new round of airstrikes against Iran Saturday evening at the direction of President Trump following the deaths of two U.S. service members.

entry.title: Colorado ICE center employee arrested after allegedly shooting protester
entry.content: An employee of a group that operates an ICE detainment center in Aurora, Colorado, was arrested after police said he shot and wounded a woman who was taking part in a protest.

entry.title: Taylor Farms recalls lettuce shipped to 27 states over cyclospora risk
entry.content: Taylor Farms has expanded a voluntary recall of its iceberg lettuce products sourced from central Mexico because of a potential link to the multistate cyclospora outbreak that has sickened people across the U.S. The California-based company said produc...

entry.title: Cuban artist and dissident exiled from country after 5 years in prison arrives in U.S.
entry.content: A famous Cuban dissident artist, Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country

entry.title: Healthcare costs associated with aging out of reach for many Americans
entry.content: One New York couple depleted their retirement and savings accounts, paying out-of-pocket for healthcare, before they were able to access Medicaid funds.

entry.title: Social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Miami
entry.content: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been arrested by federal authorities Saturday in Miami.

entry.title: The Justice Department Is Pulling Back on Prosecuting Corporate Crime
entry.content: Prosecutors have declined to charge businesses in a number of recent cases—despite believing that senior employees were involved in wrongdoing.

entry.title: Fans flock to see much-anticipated epic "The Odyssey"
entry.content: For the first time in what seems like forever, there has been a rush to see a new blockbuster on a really big screen, as Christopher Nolan's epic "The Odyssey" arrived in theaters this weekend. Noel Brennan has more.

entry.title: Taylor Farms pulls iceberg lettuce linked to cyclosporiasis outbreak
entry.content: Taylor Farms said none of its branded salads or kits contain the iceberg lettuce associated with the outbreak.

entry.title: Tate brothers arrested by U.S. authorities, face additional rape charges in the U.K.
entry.content: Social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate were taken into custody by federal law enforcement officials in Miami on Saturday, the U.S.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Big journeys begin with a single step.
Lucky Numbers: 12, 72, 30, 40, 69, 72

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Finish your workout in the morning – it's less likely to get done in the evening.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: 14% of Americans have skinny-dipped with a member of the opposite sex at least once.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Albus Dumbledore
entry.content: “The truth... It is a beautiful and terrible thing, and should therefore be treated with great caution.” — Albus Dumbledore
